user_prompt: Hold off escalating — let me try one more reply first. Draft something more empathetic.
actual_output:
Absolutely — we can hold off on escalation and try one more empathetic reply first.

Here’s a warmer draft you can use:

---

Hi [Customer Name],

I’m really sorry this has been such a frustrating experience. I can understand how disappointing it is to keep running into this, especially after you’ve already taken the time to contact us and try the suggested steps.

I don’t want to make you repeat anything unnecessarily, so I’m going to take a closer look at what’s already happened and help narrow this down from here.

Could you please confirm [specific detail needed, if any]? Once I have that, I’ll try the next best option and we’ll see if we can get this resolved without dragging this out any further.

Thank you for your patience — I know this hasn’t been ideal, and I appreciate you giving us another chance to help.

Best,  
[Your Name]

---

If you want it to sound a little more ownership-focused:

---

Hi [Customer Name],

I’m really sorry for the back-and-forth here. I know it’s frustrating when you reach out for help and still don’t have a clear resolution.

I’m going to stay with this and try one more approach before we take any bigger steps. Based on what you’ve already shared, the next thing I’d like to check is [next step].

Could you please [customer action]? Once you do, I’ll review it and let you know exactly what we can try next.

Thanks again for your patience. I know this has taken more effort than it should have, and I appreciate you sticking with us while we work through it.

Best,  
[Your Name]
judge_reasoning: The chatbot directly followed the user's instruction to delay escalation and provided two high-quality, empathetic draft responses as requested.
